items.title: Dynasty Story Engine — AI-generated clan legend that accumulates across matches
items.why: Dynasty mode exists but lacks narrative continuity. A 3-sentence 'clan legend' generated by Haiku after each match (based on actual decisions: ghost routes used, vaults captured, betrayals) creates a living saga. Players share it. Clans become characters.
items.recipe: POST /api/vaultfront/dynasty-story: pass clan slug + last 5 match outcomes + top moments → Haiku prompt → append to clan.dynasty_story (VARCHAR) in DB → render in ClanModal below dynasty emblem. Use prompt caching on system prompt.

items.title: Living Match Narrator — real-time AI broadcast commentary streamed to spectators
items.why: No game in this genre has a live AI narrator. Spectators see a scrolling commentary feed (like a sports ticker) powered by Claude Haiku with streaming. Key trigger events (convoy intercept, last-stand, heist execution) emit a 1-sentence call. Players can opt-in to text-to-speech.
items.recipe: SpectatorBus already exists. Add NarratorBus: subscribe to GameUpdateType events in Worker, batch 3 events → POST /api/vaultfront/narrator (streaming Haiku). Pipe SSE to SpectatorBus. WinModal and SpectatorAutoCamera subscribe. Rate-limit: 1 call per 15s per game.

items.title: In-Game Micro-Coach — subtle AI action hints surfaced mid-match via UIStateManager
items.why: Match-coach exists post-game only. A non-intrusive inline hint (bottom-right, 8px text, auto-dismiss 12s) powered by Haiku fires at the 2-minute mark if the player has not issued a vault command. Never pops-up — just appears. Opt-in via UserSettings. Dramatically improves new-player retention.
items.recipe: Add CoachHintEngine.ts: subscribe to UIStateManager gold+alive state, fire at tick 1200 (2 min) if no VaultFrontCommand issued → GET /api/vaultfront/micro-hint?gold=X&sites=Y → Haiku 1-sentence suggestion → render in HUD overlay z-index 90. Dedup: only 1 hint per 3 minutes.

items.title: Cinematic Intercept Moment — dramatic screen flash + animated gold-burst when convoy is intercepted
items.why: The bounty-board and convoy interception are the most exciting moments in VaultFront but they're visually underwhelming. A 0.8s PixiJS gold-particle burst at the intercept tile + HUD banner 'INTERCEPTED — +X GOLD' + a satisfying sound cue transforms the moment from mechanical to visceral.
items.recipe: Add InterceptCelebration.ts as a PixiJS layer. Subscribe to GameUpdateType.CONVOY_INTERCEPTED in ClientGameRunner. On trigger: spawn ParticleEmitter at convoy.destinationTile (gold/yellow particles, 0.8s), show HUD banner via UIStateManager.showBanner, play howler SFX 'intercept.mp3'.

items.title: Fog of War Memory Decay — tiles unseen for 60s visually fade to last-known state
items.why: Revolutionary information warfare mechanic no RTS browser game does. Tiles you visited 60s ago show ghost images of last-known troop counts with a visual desaturation effect. Players must scout. Creates entirely new strategic layer — ghost convoys become even more powerful when combined with memory fog.
items.recipe: Add tileMemoryAge: Map<TileRef, number> to TerritoryLayer. On each render tick: if tile not in current viewport, increment age; age > 600 ticks → render at 50% opacity + grayscale filter via PixiJS ColorMatrixFilter. Show 'LAST SEEN Xs AGO' tooltip on hover. Opt-in via UserSettings.

items.title: Route Intelligence Network — sell intercepted convoy routes to allies for gold
items.why: Intelligence is already the core currency of VaultFront (ghost routes, convoy prediction). Formalizing it creates a player economy. After intercepting a convoy, you can 'sell the route' to an ally — they receive a 10-tick advance warning on future convoys from that source. Ally pays you 15% of potential gold. Strategic information market.
items.recipe: Add VaultFrontCommand.SELL_INTEL: sourcePlayerID + alliancePlayerID → VaultFrontExecution checks alliance exists + last intercept within 300 ticks → grant intelActiveUntilTick to ally, debit 15% goldEstimate from future convoy, emit INTEL_SOLD GameUpdate. Add SellIntelButton to HUD post-intercept for 30s.

items.title: IGNIS Founder Feedback Loop — route founder accept/reject signals into IGNIS ranking scores
items.why: IGNIS genius hit #1 from startup brief. Currently founder feedback (task acceptance, rejection, pivots) is not fed back into IGNIS scoring. Every accepted item should increment 'founder signal' weight; every rejected item should decrement. Closes the human-in-the-loop gap that is the weakest IGNIS pillar.
items.recipe: Add POST /api/ignis/signal endpoint: {itemSlug, signal: 'accept'|'reject'|'pivot', sessionId} → write to ignis_signals table → IGNIS scoring reads weighted average of signals per pillar. Wire WinModal 'was this useful?' thumbs to this endpoint. Expose in admin dashboard.

items.title: Bot AI Persona Backstories — Haiku generates a unique bot name + 2-sentence origin story at spawn
items.why: Bots currently have personality weights but bland names. At game start, Haiku generates a unique militant codename + origin story for each bot (cached by personality+seed). Displayed in the pre-game lobby and on spectator leaderboard. Makes bots feel like rival commanders, not AI filler.
items.recipe: Extend BotSpawner.ts: on bot spawn, call GET /api/vaultfront/bot-persona?personality=X&seed=Y (cache 24h in-memory). Worker.ts adds endpoint: 20-token Haiku call, e.g. 'SHADOW MERCHANT — Economics-trained commander who built ghost-route empires through bribery'. Render in LobbyPlayerView.ts.

items.title: Spectator Economy — spectators wager spectator-gold on match outcomes, top predictors earn cosmetics
items.why: Turns passive spectating into active engagement. Spectators get 100 spectator-gold per session. They bet on outcome (player X wins, first vault captured by Y). An odds board aggregates predictions in real-time. Top weekly predictor earns a cosmetic badge. Creates a spectator metagame nobody in this genre has.
items.recipe: Add SpectatorBet table: gameId, spectatorId, prediction, amount, resolved. POST /api/spectator/bet endpoint. SpectatorBus broadcasts bet aggregates every 30s. WinModal resolves bets, credits winners via PlayerStatsStore.addSpectatorGold(). LeaderboardTabs adds SpectatorLeaderboard tab.

items.title: World Events System — server-orchestrated global events affecting all active matches in a time window
items.why: Seasonal mutator voting affects future games only. World Events affect ALL live games simultaneously for 10 minutes — e.g. 'GOLD SURGE: all convoys +50% reward for 10 min' or 'BLACKOUT: ghost route cooldown halved for all'. Creates shared 'where were you when the blackout happened' community moments.
items.recipe: Add WorldEventScheduler.ts in server: cron fires 2x/day, picks event from WorldEventConfig, broadcasts via GameManager to all active Worker instances → VaultFrontExecution.applyWorldEvent(type, durationTicks). Add world-event HUD banner (red/gold pulse). Discord announce. Store in world_events table.

items.title: Predictive Pre-Match Oracle — AI shows each player's predicted ELO change before the match starts
items.why: Pre-match tension is the most underutilized engagement window. Using player ELO history + opponent pool, Haiku generates a 'predicted outcome: +12 ELO if you win, -8 if you lose' card in GameStartingModal. Also shows 'your biggest threat: [player X]' based on matchup history. Creates stakes before a single tick fires.
items.recipe: Extend GameStartingModal.ts: on game-start event, POST /api/vaultfront/match-oracle?players=[ids] → Worker fetches ELO history from PlayerStatsStore → Haiku prompt: 'Given these ELO ratings and last 3 match outcomes, predict for each player...' → render prediction card. Expire after 30s.

items.title: Streaming Overlay API — public WebSocket endpoint for OBS/streaming overlays with sanitized match events
items.why: Twitch streamers playing VaultFront have no way to show real-time stats to viewers. A public wss://api.vaultfront.gg/stream/:gameId endpoint emits sanitized events (convoy values, territory %, ELO changes) in a format OBS browser source can consume. Community content creators become your best marketing channel.
items.recipe: Add StreamingBus.ts: subscribe to SpectatorBus events, strip PII (anonymize playerIds → stable hashes), emit JSON events to WebSocket clients on /stream/:gameId. Add StreamOverlayPage.ts (Lit component) at /overlay/:gameId — a styled HUD designed for browser-source embedding. Document in README.

items.title: In-Game Mutator Vote — move weekly mutator vote from Discord-only to in-game WinModal
items.why: Current mutator voting requires leaving the game to go to Discord. Friction kills participation. Adding a 2-button vote to WinModal (already has post-game vote prompt) means 10× more votes, better signal quality, and a completion moment that feels rewarding. Discord announce still fires, but vote source expands.
items.recipe: WinModal already calls castMutatorVote() and fetchMutatorVoteStatus(). Add renderMutatorVoteCard() that shows top 2 candidates with vote counts (from fetchMutatorVoteStatus) + vote buttons. Replace the current minimal vote prompt. Add vote-confirmation animation (green checkmark pulse).

items.title: Season Contract HUD Widget — live in-game contract progress bar visible during play
items.why: Season contracts update in WinModal post-game but players have no mid-game awareness of their progress. A compact HUD widget (top-right, 3 progress bars) showing 'CONVOY MASTER: 7/10' during play creates anticipation and directed behavior. Players chase the contract, driving session length +15% per standard engagement research.
items.recipe: Add ContractHudWidget.ts as a Lit component. Mount in ClientGameRunner after game-start. Fetch from /api/vaultfront/contracts (already exists) → render 3 most-in-progress contracts as horizontal progress bars with label + count. Update locally on each VaultFrontStatusUpdate event. Collapse to icon on mobile.

items.title: Automated Anti-Cheat Alerts — Discord/email alert when anti-cheat signals exceed threshold
items.why: The anti-cheat admin endpoint exists but requires manual polling. Automated alerting when getFlaggedMatches() returns >3 new flags in 1 hour converts reactive to proactive moderation. Security posture requires timely response; manual polling guarantees latency.
items.recipe: Add AntiCheatMonitor.ts to server: setInterval 30min, call PlayerStatsStore.getFlaggedMatches(last30min), if count > threshold → POST to DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL (already in DiscordNotifier) with flagged player list + signal types. Add ANTI_CHEAT_ALERT_THRESHOLD env var (default: 3).

items.title: Match Replay Highlight Reel — auto-generated shareable highlight sequence from top ReplayMoments
items.why: ReplayHighlightStore and shareReplayHighlight() exist but the UX is a single-clip share. Stringing the top 3 ReplayMoments from a match into an auto-play sequence (like NFL top plays) creates a 'relive the best moments' feature that drives organic social sharing. 30s highlight reel is the natural shareable unit.
items.recipe: Add ReplayReelBuilder.ts: fetch top 3 moments from ReplayHighlightStore by 'scope:global' + sort by engagement. Add 'Watch Highlights' button in WinModal → opens ReplayPanel in reel mode: auto-advance between moments with 1.5s transition. Add shareReplayReel() to Api.ts: creates /replay/:id?mode=reel link.

items.title: Narrative Batch Caching — consolidate 5 per-match AI calls into 1 batched Haiku request with prefix caching
items.why: Currently: battle-narrative + prophecy + commentary + lore + mission = 5 separate API calls per match end. Combined system prompt is identical for all 5. Batching into a single multi-task request with prompt caching reduces cost ~60% and latency by 4 round-trips. Measurement: context-meter ledger before/after.
items.recipe: Add NarrativeBatchWorker.ts: on game end, collect all 5 narrative inputs → single Haiku request with cached system prompt (use anthropic-beta: prompt-caching-2024-07-31). Prompt: 'Return JSON with keys: narrative, prophecy, commentary, mission, lore based on match data below'. Split response fields to existing handlers.

items.title: Clan War Tournament — auto-seeded elimination bracket for top-16 ELO clans, weekly cadence
items.why: Clan ELO now exists but has no competitive destination. A weekly auto-seeded elimination bracket for top-16 clans by ELO creates a structured competitive season. Games are normal VaultFront matches; the bracket tracks clan ELO results. Winner gets Discord role + cosmetic. Creates a metagame layer above individual matches.
items.recipe: Extend TournamentStore.ts: add auto-seed logic from ClanStore.getLeaderboard(limit:16). Add POST /api/clan-war/seed (cron-triggered Monday midnight) that creates bracket. Add ClanWarBracketPage.ts Lit component (bracket visualization using existing LeaderboardTabs pattern). Auto-announce rounds via DiscordNotifier.

items.title: AB Test Results Dashboard — admin page surfacing dock/recap/runtime experiment outcomes
items.why: AB infrastructure exists (dock/recap/runtime assignment + event recording) but results require raw DB queries. A simple admin dashboard page (admin-only route) that shows conversion rates per variant turns the investment into actionable insights. Without this, AB tests are write-only black boxes.
items.recipe: Add GET /api/admin/ab/results: aggregate dock_events/recap_events/runtime_events tables by variant, compute conversion rate + confidence interval (Wilson score). Add AbDashboard.ts Lit page at /admin/ab — protected by Privilege.ADMIN. Table: variant | events | conversions | rate | 95% CI.

skipped.reason: Priority 6.96 — organizational refactor with no player-facing impact; defer to debt sweep session
Helmet.js already in dependencies; one-liner config, not worth a full audit item
